texto: Washingtonians love the "So-and-so is spinning in his grave" cliché. Someone is always speculating about how some great dead American would be scandalized over some crime against How It Used to Be. The Founding Fathers are always spinning in their graves over something, as is Ronald Reagan, or FDR. Edward R. Murrow is a perennial grave spinner in the news business (though in fact, Murrow was cremated).
explicacao: Citação de Mark Leibovich sobre vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. “Washingtonians love the "So-and-so is spinning in his grave" cliché. Someone is always speculatin…” articula familia, cliches, comparison em registro de familia, com relevância para contextos como comparison e contrasts.
